steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The blank follows a gerund phrase that acts as the sentence’s subject: Distinguishing exactly the same and nearly the same commodities—whether crops, lumber, or precious metals—. A gerund phrase is singular, so the verb must agree with a singular subject.

steps.title: Apply subject‑verb agreement
steps.content:
A singular subject takes a singular verb; only was provides a singular past‑tense verb that matches the subject.

The other choices either use a plural verb (were), a participle that cannot act as a finite verb (having been, being), or a non‑finite form, breaking subject‑verb agreement.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
